info.name: Global Control API
info.description:
# Global Control API

The Global Control API provides a comprehensive interface for managing AI bot configurations, user interactions, and system settings. This API enables you to control various aspects of your AI bot ecosystem including conversation management, user data handling, and system configuration.

## Base URL

```
https://api.globalcontrol.io/api

 ```

## Authentication

This API uses **API Key authentication**. All requests must include your API token in the `X-API-KEY` header.

### Header Format

```
X-API-KEY: {{apiToken}}

 ```

### How to Get Your API Token

1. Log in to your Global Control dashboard
    
2. Navigate to Settings > API Access
    
3. Generate a new API token or copy your existing token
    
4. Store the token securely - it will be used for all API requests
    

### Using the API Token in Postman

This collection is configured to use API Key authentication at the collection level. All requests automatically inherit this authentication method.

The token is stored in the collection variable `{{apiToken}}`. To set your token:

1. Click on the collection name
    
2. Go to the "Variables" tab
    
3. Set the current value for `apiToken` to your actual API token
    
4. Save the collection
    

All requests will automatically include the `X-API-KEY` header with your token value.

## Key Features

- **User Management**: Create, update, and manage user profiles and preferences
    
- **Conversation Control**: Handle chat sessions, message history, and conversation flows
    
- **Bot Configuration**: Configure AI bot behavior, responses, and capabilities
    
- **Analytics & Monitoring**: Track usage, performance metrics, and system health
    
- **Webhook Integration**: Set up real-time notifications and event handling
    

## Rate Limiting

The API implements rate limiting to ensure fair usage:

- Standard tier: 100 requests per minute
    
- Premium tier: 500 requests per minute
    
- Enterprise tier: Custom limits available
    

Rate limit information is included in response headers:

- `X-RateLimit-Limit`: Maximum requests allowed
    
- `X-RateLimit-Remaining`: Requests remaining in current window
    
- `X-RateLimit-Reset`: Time when the rate limit resets (Unix timestamp)
    

## Response Format

All API responses follow a consistent JSON structure:

### Success Response

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    // Response data here
  },
  "message": "Operation completed successfully"
}

 ```

### Error Response

``` json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "ERROR_CODE",
    "message": "Human-readable error message",
    "details": {}
  }
}

 ```

## Common HTTP Status Codes

- `200 OK`: Request succeeded
    
- `201 Created`: Resource created successfully
    
- `400 Bad Request`: Invalid request parameters
    
- `401 Unauthorized`: Missing or invalid API token
    
- `403 Forbidden`: Insufficient permissions
    
- `404 Not Found`: Resource not found
    
- `429 Too Many Requests`: Rate limit exceeded
    
- `500 Internal Server Error`: Server error occurred
    

## Support

For API support, documentation, or to report issues:

- Email: [support@ammadsarfraz.com](https://mailto:support@ammadsarfraz.com)
    
- Documentation: [https://docs.globalcontrol.ammadsarfraz.com](https://docs.globalcontrol.ammadsarfraz.com)
    
- Status Page: [https://status.globalcontrol.ammadsarfraz.com](https://status.globalcontrol.ammadsarfraz.com)
    

## Versioning

This API uses URL versioning. The current version is v1. Breaking changes will be introduced in new versions while maintaining backward compatibility for existing versions.

Version format: `/v{version_number}/endpoint`

## Best Practices

1. **Secure Your API Token**: Never expose your API token in client-side code or public repositories
    
2. **Handle Rate Limits**: Implement exponential backoff when rate limits are reached
    
3. **Error Handling**: Always check the `success` field and handle errors appropriately
    
4. **Use Webhooks**: For real-time updates, use webhooks instead of polling
    
5. **Cache Responses**: Cache responses when appropriate to reduce API calls

                            "code": "let options = {\n\tmethod: 'post',\n\turl: `https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/${bundle.inputData.calender_id}/events`,\n\theaders: {\n\t\t'Accept': 'application/json',\n\t\t'Authorization': `Bearer ${bundle.authData.access_token}`,\n\t\t'content-type': 'application/json',\n\t},\n\tbody: {\n        summary: bundle.inputData.summary,\n        description: bundle.inputData.description,\n    \n        start: {\n          dateTime: bundle.inputData.start_date,\n          timeZone: bundle.inputData.timezone,\n        },\n        end: {\n          dateTime: bundle.inputData.end_date,\n          timeZone: bundle.inputData.timezone,\n        },\n    \n        // Busy\n        transparency: bundle.inputData.transparency || \"opaque\",\n    \n        // Default calendar visibility\n        visibility: bundle.inputData.visibility || \"default\",\n    \n        // Matches your checkbox UI\n        guestsCanModify: true,\n        guestsCanInviteOthers: true,\n        guestsCanSeeOtherGuests: true,\n    \n        // 10 min reminder\n        reminders: {\n          useDefault: false,\n          overrides: [{ method: \"popup\", minutes: 10 }],\n        },\n    \n        // ONLY the contact\n        attendees: bundle.inputData.attendees || [],\n        \n        source: bundle.inputData.source || {\n          title: \"Global Control\",\n          url: \"https://app.globalcontrol.io\"\n        },\n        \n        extendedProperties: {\n            private: {\n              booking_source: \"Global Control\"\n            }\n          }\n      },\n}\nreturn fetch.request(options).then(res => {\n\treturn res;\n});",
                            "description": "",
                            "method": "post",
                            "url": "https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/{{bundle.inputData.calender_id}}/events"

                            "code": "function normalizeEmail(email) {\n  return email.trim().toLowerCase();\n}\n\n\nvar email = normalizeEmail('{{bundle.inputData.email}}') \nlet eData=hash(email).toLowerCase()\n\nlet options = {\n\tmethod: 'post',\n\turl: 'https://graph.facebook.com/v21.0/{{bundle.inputData.audience_id}}/users',\n\theaders: {\n\t\t'Accept': 'application/json',\n\t\t'content-type': 'application/json',\n\t},\n\tbody:{\n\t    \n        \"payload\": {\n        \"schema\": \"EMAIL\",\n        \"data\": [eData]\n      },\n      \"access_token\": \"{{bundle.authData.access_token}}\"\n\t},\n}\nreturn fetch.request(options).then(res => {\n\treturn res;\n});",

                            "code": "const topic = bundle.inputData.topic || \"Scheduled Meeting\";\nconst isWebinar = bundle.inputData.is_webinar || false;\nconst options = {\n  method: \"post\",\n  url: \"https://api.zoom.us/v2/users/me/meetings\",\n  headers: {\n    Accept: \"application/json\",\n    Authorization: `Bearer ${bundle.authData.access_token}`,\n    \"content-type\": \"application/json\",\n  },\n  body: {\n    topic,\n    type: isWebinar ? 5 : 2, // 5 = webinar, 2 = scheduled meeting\n    start_time: bundle.inputData.start_time,\n    duration: bundle.inputData.duration || 30,\n    timezone: bundle.inputData.timezone || \"America/Los_Angeles\",\n    agenda: topic,\n    default_password: true,\n    password: bundle.inputData.password || \"123456\",\n    settings: {\n      host_video: true,\n      focus_mode: true,\n      question_answer: true,\n      allow_multiple_devices: true,\n      email_notification: true,\n      participant_video: false,\n      disable_participant_video: true,\n      mute_upon_entry: true,\n      join_before_host: false,\n      allow_host_control_participant_mute_state: true,\n      waiting_room: true,\n      allow_multiple_devices: true,\n      panelists_video: true,\n      practice_session: true,\n      hd_video: true,\n      auto_recording: \"cloud\",\n\n      question_and_answer: {\n        enable: true,\n        allow_submit_questions: true,\n        allow_anonymous_questions: false,\n        question_visibility: \"all\",\n        attendees_can_comment: true,\n        attendees_can_upvote: true,\n      },\n    },\n  },\n};\n\nreturn fetch.request(options).then((res) => {\n  return res;\n});\n",
                            "description": "",
                            "method": "post",
                            "url": "https://api.zoom.us/v2/users/me/meetings"
                        },

                            "code": "// const channelName = \"GlobalControl38\";\r\nvar channelName = \"{{bundle.authData.channel_name}}\".replace(/ /g, \"_\")+\"{{bundle.authData.api_key}}\".slice(-5);\r\nlet promises=[];\r\nconst webhookUrls = {\r\n  received: `https://api.globalcontrol.io/webhooks/email/accepted?provider=smtp.com`,\r\n  delivered: `https://api.globalcontrol.io/webhooks/email/delivered?provider=smtp.com`,\r\n  opened: `https://api.globalcontrol.io/webhooks/email/opened?provider=smtp.com`,\r\n  clicked: `https://api.globalcontrol.io/webhooks/email/clicked?provider=smtp.com`,\r\n  complained: `https://api.globalcontrol.io/webhooks/email/complained?provider=smtp.com`,\r\n  failed: `https://api.globalcontrol.io/webhooks/email/failed?provider=smtp.com`,\r\n  unsubscribed: `https://api.globalcontrol.io/webhooks/email/unsubscribed?provider=smtp.com`,\r\n};\r\n\r\nconst results = [];\r\nconst keys = Object.keys(webhookUrls);\r\n\r\n// Step 1: Start with the create-channel request\r\nlet chain = fetch.request({\r\n  method: \"post\",\r\n  url: \"https://api.smtp.com/v4/channels\",\r\n  headers: {\r\n    Accept: \"application/json\",\r\n    \"content-type\": \"application/json\",\r\n    Authorization: \"Basic \" + fetch.encodeBase64(\"api:{{bundle.authData.api_key}}\"),\r\n  },\r\n  body: {\r\n    name: channelName,\r\n    smtp_username: channelName,\r\n    smtp_password: channelName,\r\n    quota: 0,\r\n  },\r\n})\r\n.then(response => {\r\n  results.push({ key: \"create_channel\", status: \"success\", response });\r\n})\r\n.catch(error => {\r\n  results.push({ key: \"create_channel\", status: \"error\", error });\r\n})\r\n.then(wait(15000)); // \r\n\r\n// Step 2: Chain webhooks sequentially with wait\r\nkeys.forEach((key, index) => {\r\n  chain = chain\r\n    .then(() => {\r\n      const webhookOptions = {\r\n        method: \"post\",\r\n        url: `https://api.smtp.com/v4/callbacks/${key}?channel=${channelName}&medium=http&address=${webhookUrls[key]}`,\r\n        headers: {\r\n          Accept: \"application/json\",\r\n          Authorization: \"Basic \" + fetch.encodeBase64(\"api:{{bundle.authData.api_key}}\"),\r\n          \"content-type\": \"application/json\",\r\n        },\r\n      };\r\n      return fetch.request(webhookOptions);\r\n    })\r\n    .then(response => {\r\n      results.push({ key, status: \"success\", response });\r\n    })\r\n    .catch(error => {\r\n      results.push({ key, status: \"error\", error });\r\n    });\r\n\r\n  if (index < keys.length - 1) {\r\n    chain = chain.then(wait(1000)); // 🔥 wait between webhooks\r\n  }\r\n});\r\n\r\n// Step 3: Combine with other promises after everything is done\r\nreturn chain.then(() => {\r\n  return Promise.all([...promises]).then(otherResults => {\r\n    return [...otherResults, ...results];\r\n  });\r\n});",

                            "code": "var channelName = `${bundle.authData.channel_name}`.replace(/ /g, \"_\") + `${bundle.authData.api_key}`.slice(-5);\n\nlet options = {\n  method: \"post\",\n  url: \"https://api.smtp.com/v4/messages\",\n  headers: {\n    Accept: \"application/json\",\n    \"content-type\": \"application/json\",\n    Authorization: \"Basic \" + fetch.encodeBase64(`api:${bundle.authData.api_key}`),\n  },\n  body: {\n    channel: `${bundle.inputData.channel || channelName}`, // Your SMTP.com channel name\n    recipients: {\n      to: [\n        {\n          address: `${bundle.inputData.to_email}`,\n          name: `${bundle.inputData.to_name}`,\n        },\n      ],\n    },\n    originator: {\n      from: {\n        address: `${bundle.inputData.from_email}`,\n        name: `${bundle.inputData.from_name}`,\n      },\n      ...(bundle.inputData.reply_to && {\n        reply_to: {\n          address: `${bundle.inputData.reply_to}`,\n        },\n      }),\n    },\n    subject: replaceShortcodes(bundle.inputData.subject),\n    body: {\n      parts: [\n        {\n          version: \"1.0\",\n          type: \"text/html\",\n          charset: \"UTF-8\",\n          content: replaceShortcodes(bundle.inputData.content),\n        },\n      ],\n      custom_headers: {\n        \"X-Domain\": `${bundle.inputData.domain}`,\n      },\n    },\n  },\n};\n\nreturn fetch.request(options).then((res) => {\n  const id = res.data.message.match(/msg_id:\\s*([^\\s]+)/)[1];\n  return {\n    ...res,\n    meta: {\n      id: id,\n    },\n  };\n});\n",
